items.title: Dow Jones Futures: Trump Threatens 100% Canada Tariff; Tesla, Microsoft, Meta, Apple Lead Earnings Wave
items.description: President Donald Trump threatened a 100% tariff on Canada if it makes a trade deal with China, a week after endorsing the idea. Tesla, Microsoft, Meta and Apple lead an earnings wave.

items.title: Apple, Microsoft, Meta To Report Results; Fed's Interest Rate Decision In Focus
items.description: Wall Street Week Ahead: key earnings (AAPL, MSFT, META, TSLA), Fed rate decision, IPOs, dividends and volatility picks. Read more details here.

items.title: Mag 7 earnings, Fed's rate decision: What to Watch
items.description: Market Domination Overtime Host Josh Lipton previews several of the biggest stories to come throughout next week, including a wide array of earnings results due out from major companies like Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), Apple (AAPL), Tesla (TSLA), and many more; and the Federal Reserve's latest interest rate decision followed by a press conference with chairman Jerome Powell. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

items.title: DIA vs. IWM: DIA Combines Higher Yield With Lower Cost, While IWM Offers Greater Diversification
items.author: Jake Lerch
items.description: DIA tracks 30 blue-chip stocks with lower costs, higher dividend yield, and less volatility, while IWM provides broader diversification across 1,954 small-cap stocks. DIA showed stronger 5-year returns and risk-adjusted metrics, but IWM delivered higher 1-year returns. The choice depends on investor preference for stability versus diversification.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: DIA is presented favorably with lower expense ratio (0.16%), higher dividend yield (1.4%), lower volatility (beta 0.91), better 5-year returns ($1,749 vs $1,341), and smaller max drawdown (-20.76% vs -31.91%). The article suggests it appeals to investors seeking stability and established companies.

IWM is presented as a viable alternative with strengths in diversification (1,954 holdings) and higher 1-year returns (20.0% vs 18.1%), but with higher volatility (beta 1.13) and larger drawdowns. It appeals to investors prioritizing broad small-cap exposure over stability.
Mentioned as a top holding in DIA (3.28% position). Neutral mention as it's simply identified as a major constituent without specific performance commentary.
